segments.text: This is how economic size changed across the world, from 1960 to 2024.
At the start, United States leads, with Germany and United Kingdom right behind.
In 1971, the front of the table is United States, Germany, and Japan.
In 1981, the front of the table is United States, Japan, and Germany.
In 1992, the front of the table is United States, Japan, and Germany.
In 2003, the front of the table is United States, Japan, and Germany.
In 2013, the front of the table is United States, China, and Japan.
By 2024, the leading names are United States, China, Germany.
The big story here is how the United States stayed on top while China and India changed the table underneath it.
Follow Datavault for more animated data stories driven by real numbers.
